content.text:
[Fri 2026-04-17 15:34 EDT] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status. [Subagent Task]: Fix Recipe Rip so emojis display CENTERED ON THEIR OWN LINE BELOW the recipe title. ## Problem The emojis appear to the RIGHT of the title on the same line. They need to be on a SEPARATE line, centered. ## File ~/agent-mission-control/dist-recipe/index.html ## Current HTML Structure The detail view has something like: ```html

Title

emojis
``` ## Current CSS ```css .rth{display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;margin-bottom:12px} .rt{display:block;font-size:22px;font-weight:700;line-height:1.25;margin-bottom:4px;text-align:center} .re{display:block;text-align:center;font-size:28px;margin-top:8px;letter-spacing:4px} ``` ## What Should Happen Title: "Lemon Chicken Orzo Soup" (centered) Emojis: 🍋🐔🥣 (on their own line, centered below title) ## Your Task 1. Read the file 2. Check the HTML structure in the detail view render code 3. Make sure emojis render AFTER the title h1 tag, on a separate line 4. Make sure CSS properly separates them - the key is `.re` needs to be a block-level element that forces a new line 5. The h1 should NOT have `float` or `inline` issues 6. Copy to source: cp ~/agent-mission-control/dist-recipe/index.html ~/agent-mission-control/recipe-rip/index.html 7. Commit ## Critical - Title on one line - Emojis on the NEXT line, centered - They must NOT be on the same visual line - Don't break anything else Report what you found and fixed!

content.thinking:
**Inspecting layout issues**

I'm inspecting potential style issues that might be causing something to stay on the same line. It could be that the h1 is closed before the div or perhaps there’s a flex column not stacking correctly. The display properties of h1 could also influence this, especially if it has default margins or inline styles. Maybe wrapping the emoji in a separate div and adjusting some styles could help, so I'll go ahead and edit.

details.explanation: Adjusted detail-view markup/CSS, copied the built file to the source mirror, and committed the tracked change.

plan.step: Inspect Recipe Rip detail view HTML and CSS in dist file
Patch layout so emoji line renders below centered title
Copy updated file to source and commit changes

content.text:
Found that the detail view HTML already rendered emojis after the `<h1>`, so the issue was layout resilience, not element order.

What I fixed:
- Kept the emoji block explicitly after the title in the detail render code.
- Strengthened the CSS so the title and emoji line cannot sit on the same visual row:
  - `.rth` now has full width and centered text
  - `.rt` now has `width:100%`, `margin:0`, `float:none`, `clear:both`
  - `.re` now has `display:block`, `width:100%`, `flex:0 0 100%`, `clear:both`
- Added `aria-label="Recipe emojis"` to the emoji line

Files:
- Updated: `~/agent-mission-control/dist-recipe/index.html`
- Copied to: `~/agent-mission-control/recipe-rip/index.html`

Commit:
- `222467d` - `Fix recipe title emoji stacking`

Relevant note:
- `recipe-rip/` is gitignored in that repo, so the copied source file was updated on disk but not included in the commit. The tracked `dist-recipe/index.html` change is committed successfully.
